capabilities_attempted_but_failed: python3 for byte verification (not installed in container)
xxd / hexdump for hex inspection (not installed in container)
what_worked_well: Broker process NEVER died across every malformed payload and every bogus --at/--delay I threw at it (12 bogus time-args + NUL + binary + unterminated payloads) - the fork-per-connection isolation genuinely contains crashes.
Normal traffic keeps working AFTER a malformed barrage: a clean immediate publish (NORMAL-NOW) and a clean --delay 2 (DELAYED-2S) both delivered exactly once. Recovery is real.

The data-derived MIN(run_at) scheduler is unbothered by a garbage entry: with a year-2286 deferred file (9999999999000.x) sitting in deferred/, a near-term --delay 2 still fired on time. No forever-firing, no busy-spin: /proc scheduler ticks were 0-1 over 3s windows even with junk pending.
Crashed handlers did NOT leak their malformed payloads to subscribers (junk count=0, far count=0 in sub.out) - the bad publish dies in its own forked handler.
Newline-terminated text payloads with embedded internal newlines deliver byte-for-byte identical (cmp IDENTICAL) - line-oriented delivery itself is faithful for text.
wished_for: A real length-prefix wire framing so binary/NUL/unterminated payloads survive - or, if line-framing is the actual design, the docs should SAY 'newline-delimited text only' and drop the 'content-blind' wording.

Clean rejection of bogus --at/--delay: a single-line error back to the client and rc!=0, instead of a raw bash 'unbound variable'/'value too great for base'/'invalid arithmetic operator' spew into broker.log plus 'socat E waitpid child exited status 1'.
A documented bound or validation on --at (negatives, far-future, non-numeric) and a stated unit (it interprets --at as SECONDS and multiplies to ms - undocumented).
Tools in the dev container for users who verify bytes: od/cmp/perl are there but python3/xxd/hexdump are missing, which slowed verification.
evidence_log: FRAMING byte-exact (.eval-scratch/scripter-byteexact.sh): 'nul-bytes' sent=7 recv=0 DIFFER (cmp: EOF, sub.out empty); 'binary-16KB' sent=16384 recv=16006 DIFFER at char 128; 'binary-64KB' sent=65536 recv=65201 DIFFER at char 225; 'embedded-newlines-notrail' sent=17 recv=12 DIFFER (last line 'line3' dropped); control 'embedded-newlines-trail' sent=18 recv=18 IDENTICAL.

FRAMING diagnosis (.eval-scratch/scripter-diagnose.sh) on a deterministic 0x00..0xff ramp: sent NUL count=64 -> recv NUL count=0 (ALL nuls stripped); newline count 64->64 preserved; other bytes 64->63 (last unterminated line lost); verdict 'DIFFERENT MULTISET (bytes lost/changed)' NOT a reorder.

FRAMING confirm (.eval-scratch/scripter-frame-confirm.sh): NUL 'A\0B\0C' recv=0; 'tail-no-newline' (15 bytes, no \n) recv=0 i.e. a single unterminated line delivers NOTHING; control 'ok-with-newline\n' IDENTICAL. => framing is newline-TERMINATED line delivery, no length-prefix at the wire.

BOGUS --at (.eval-scratch/scripter-bogus-at2.sh): broker alive=yes throughout; --at 1 / --at 1577836800 / --at -5 / --delay -3 / --delay 0 -> delivered immediately (treated as past/now); --at 9999999999 / 99999999999999 -> staged in deferred/ as 9999999999000.x / 99999999999999000.x (interpreted as seconds*1000, year 2286 / ~year 5.1M) and persist forever.

BOGUS --at CRASHES (broker.log): 'line 175: abc: unbound variable'; 'line 175: 12ab34: value too great for base'; 'line 175: 1700000000.5: syntax error: invalid arithmetic operator (error token is ".5")'; 'line 164: $2: unbound variable' (empty --at); 'line 174/175: xyz: unbound variable' (--delay xyz) - each followed by '2026/... socat[N] E waitpid(): child M exited with status 1'. pub client still returned rc=0.

RESILIENCE (.eval-scratch/scripter-resilience.sh): after a 5-way malformed barrage + a far-future deferred entry, broker alive=yes; NORMAL-NOW count=1, DELAYED-2S count=1 both delivered; leaked junk count=0, far count=0; scheduler ticks over 3s = 1 (idle) while year-2286 entry pending; --delay 2 fired within the expected window (4.03s total incl. sleep 3).

friction_points.description: Framing is newline-TERMINATED line delivery via read, NOT length-prefix framing. NUL bytes (0x00) are stripped entirely, arbitrary binary is mangled (16KB sent=16384 recv=16006, multiset differs), and a single payload with no trailing newline delivers ZERO bytes. This directly contradicts the README/DEMO 'content-blind' claim and the challenge's 'binary/NUL deliver intact under length-prefix framing' success criterion.
friction_points.evidence: scripter-byteexact.sh: nul-bytes recv=0; binary-16KB DIFFER@128. scripter-diagnose.sh: NUL 64->0, DIFFERENT MULTISET. scripter-frame-confirm.sh: 'tail-no-newline' recv=0.
friction_points.workaround: Only publish newline-terminated, NUL-free text. base64/hex-encode any binary before pub and decode in the subscriber pipeline. Always append a trailing \n or the message vanishes.

friction_points.description: Non-numeric/float/empty --at and non-numeric --delay are NOT rejected cleanly - they crash the per-connection handler with raw bash diagnostics ('unbound variable', 'value too great for base', 'invalid arithmetic operator') logged to broker.log, plus 'socat E waitpid child exited status 1'. The pub client still exits rc=0, so a scripter gets a silent server-side failure with no error returned and no scriptable signal.
friction_points.evidence: scripter-bogus-at2.sh broker.log: 'line 175: abc: unbound variable', 'line 175: 1700000000.5: syntax error: invalid arithmetic operator', 'line 164: $2: unbound variable'; pub rc=0 in every case.
friction_points.workaround: Pre-validate --at/--delay are plain integers in the calling script before invoking pub; never trust pub's exit code for time-arg validity.

friction_points.description: Far-future and negative --at values are accepted without bound or unit-checking. --at 9999999999 and --at 99999999999999 are interpreted as SECONDS, multiplied to ms (year 2286 / year ~5.1M), and parked in deferred/ forever; negative --at and --delay 0/-3 are treated as fire-now. The --at unit (seconds) is undocumented.
friction_points.evidence: scripter-bogus-at2.sh: deferred/ holds 9999999999000.284711033 and 99999999999999000.643028060 after wait; --at 1 and --at -5 delivered immediately.
friction_points.workaround: Validate --at against a sane epoch range in the calling script; document that --at expects unix seconds.

friction_points.description: The dev container ships od/cmp/perl/dd but NOT python3/xxd/hexdump. A user verifying byte-exactness of payloads (the natural test for a 'content-blind' broker) has to fall back to od/perl, which slows the exact work this product invites.
friction_points.evidence: scripter-tools.sh: MISS xxd, MISS hexdump, MISS python3, MISS python; HAVE od/cmp/perl.
friction_points.workaround: Use od -An -tx1 and cmp -s; write byte-comparison logic in perl instead of python.

free_text:
As the scripter/integrator I ran the broker in the shellmux-dev container against my whole abuse playbook (scripts under .eval-scratch/scripter-*.sh). Two clean verdicts.

RESILIENCE HALF: PASS. The broker never died - 12 bogus --at/--delay variants, NUL payloads, multi-KB binary, unterminated lines, all of it - and normal traffic kept flowing afterward (NORMAL-NOW and DELAYED-2S each delivered once). The actual contribution, the deadline scheduler, shrugs off garbage: a year-2286 deferred entry sat in deferred/ without blocking a near-term --delay 2 from firing on time, and scheduler CPU stayed at 0-1 ticks/3s (no busy-spin, no forever-firing). Fork-per-connection genuinely contains a crashing handler - the malformed payloads never leaked to subscribers. Good.

FRAMING HALF: FAIL against the documented claim. This is the headline. shellmux does NOT do length-prefix framing; it does newline-TERMINATED line delivery. Proven byte-exact three ways: (1) NUL bytes are stripped wholesale (64 sent -> 0 received on a deterministic ramp); (2) arbitrary binary is mangled (16KB in, 16006 out, different multiset - not a reorder, real loss); (3) a payload with no trailing newline delivers ZERO bytes ('tail-no-newline', 15 bytes -> recv=0). The challenge success criterion 'Binary/newline/NUL payloads deliver intact under length-prefix framing' is observably false, and README line 3 / DEMO line 3 'content-blind' is misleading: the host doesn't just SEE every byte, it EATS some of them. For an integrator the honest contract is 'newline-delimited NUL-free text only' - say that and I can work with it; promise content-blind and I'll lose data silently.

SECONDARY: bogus --at/--delay aren't rejected, they crash the handler with raw bash 'unbound variable'/'arithmetic' errors into broker.log while pub still returns rc=0 - a silent server-side failure with no scriptable signal. And --at silently accepts far-future/negative/unit-ambiguous values.

Net: the broker is robust (stays up, recovers, scheduler unbothered) but its data-integrity contract is narrower than advertised. The challenge is well-fitted - it exposed exactly the gap between the 'content-blind / length-prefix' marketing and the line-read reality. Relevant artifacts: /Users/bobbyrathore/Documents/WildProjects/cool-oss-projects/hackathon-ideas/projects/shellmux/.eval-scratch/scripter-byteexact.sh, scripter-diagnose.sh, scripter-frame-confirm.sh, scripter-bogus-at2.sh, scripter-resilience.sh.
